diagrams.title: Stakeholder Power/Interest Grid
diagrams.description: A 2x2 matrix classifying stakeholders based on their power (influence over project) and interest (level of concern about project outcomes)
elements: Top-Right: High Power + High Interest - Manage Closely (Key Players)
Top-Left: High Power + Low Interest - Keep Satisfied
Bottom-Right: Low Power + High Interest - Keep Informed
Bottom-Left: Low Power + Low Interest - Monitor
diagrams.purpose: Helps prioritize communication and negotiation efforts with different stakeholders based on their influence and involvement

diagrams.title: Kano Model Satisfaction Curve
diagrams.description: Graph showing relationship between requirement fulfillment and customer satisfaction for three types of requirements
elements: Basic/Must-be: Step function - flat line at bottom, sudden drop when missing
Performance: Diagonal line - linear increase with more fulfillment
Attractive/Delighters: Exponentially increasing curve - high satisfaction when present, no dissatisfaction when absent
diagrams.purpose: Understanding which features drive customer satisfaction vs expected baseline features

diagrams.title: Negotiation Zone (ZOPA)
diagrams.description: Visual representation of the Zone of Possible Agreement showing ranges for each party
elements: Party A Walk-away (BATNA) <-- ZOPA (Range) --> Party B Walk-away (BATNA)
Party A Target (Ideal) <-- ZOPA center --> Party B Target (Ideal)
No agreement zone outside ZOPA where parties cannot meet
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing overlap between parties' acceptable outcomes to identify achievable agreements

diagrams.title: Requirements Prioritization Funnel
diagrams.description: Shows filtering process from all requirements to final scope using MoSCoW and triage
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how requirements are filtered to fit project constraints and priorities
